---
title: Squared coefficient of variation
jupyter: python3
---

### Set up and load data

```{python}

## Import modules
import bct
import abct
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from scipy import io

## Load data
data = io.loadmat("./data/hcp_data.mat")

```

### Set up data and functions

```{python}

# Get data
Wn = abct.kneighbors(data["W"], "common", kappa=0.1).toarray()
Cn = abct.kneighbors(data["X"].T @ data["X"], "common", kappa=0.1).toarray()

# Get squared coefficient of variation
WV = abct.coefvar2(Wn)[1]
CV = abct.coefvar2(Cn)[1]

# Get participation coefficients
K = np.r_[5:30:5]          # number of clusters
repl = 10                  # number of replicates

### Run Loyvain k-modularity
np.random.seed(1)
WP = [None] * len(K)
CP = [None] * len(K)
for i, k in enumerate(K):
    print(f"Number of clusters: {k}")
    Mw = abct.loyvain(Wn, k, "kmodularity", replicates=repl)[0]
    Mc = abct.loyvain(Cn, k, "kmodularity", replicates=repl)[0]
    WP[i] = bct.participation_coef(Wn, Mw)
    CP[i] = bct.participation_coef(Cn, Mc)

```

### Scatter plots of CV2 and participation coefficient

```{python}

fig = px.scatter(
    labels={"x": "Participation coefficient", "y": "- (Coefficient of variation)^2"},
    width=600, 
    height=600,
    log_x=True,
)

for i in range(len(K)):
    fig.add_scatter(
        x = 1 - WP[i],
        y = - WV,
        mode="markers",
    )
fig.update_layout(title_x=0.5, showlegend=False).show()


fig = px.scatter(
    labels={"x": "Participation coefficient", "y": "- (Coefficient of variation)^2"},
    width=600, 
    height=600,
    log_x=True,
)

for i in range(len(K)):
    fig.add_scatter(
        x = 1 - CP[i],
        y = - CV,
        mode="markers",
    )
fig.update_layout(title_x=0.5, showlegend=False).show()

```
